title: FormSubmission
description: One completed (or in-progress) submission of a FormVersion. `answers` JSONB validated against the version's schema at submit time. Save-and-resume via `session_signature` — the resumer follows an emailed link that carries the signed session URL, so the same person can pause + finish the form without a login. Sensitive answers (medical, safeguarding-adjacent) are encrypted at rest per the tenant's AnswerCipher key. Handoff wiring — on completion, if the parent Form declares a `handoff_slug`, RunFormHandoffJob (or sync path when async=false) executes the registered #[AsFormHandoff] handler → creates the target domain entity (e.g. Registration).

status.description: in_progress / completed / abandoned / failed_validation. TTL-swept sessions become `abandoned`.

session_signature.description: Signed URL segment for save-and-resume. Rotates on session-key rotation. Non-enumerable, >= 64 chars entropy.

session_expires_at.description: Session TTL. Default 72h from start — configurable per form via Form.settings.session_ttl_hours.

resumer_user_id.description: Set when a logged-in user submits (staff-assisted intake). Null for pure public submissions.

submitter_email_snapshot.description: Snapshot of the primary email captured on the form. Used to send resume-session email + notification email. Redacted for platform-admin audit reads.

locale.description: The locale the form was submitted in (BCP-47).

answers.description: Field key → value map. Validated against the FormVersion.schema at submit. Sensitive fields (medical / safeguarding-classified) encrypted at rest via AnswerCipher.

answers_hash.description: SHA-256 of the answers JSON at submit — for tamper detection + dedup.

validation_errors.description: Last-known validation errors (field_key → error_message[]). Retained on status=failed_validation for user retry.

handoff_status.description: not_applicable / pending / running / succeeded / failed. Null when Form has no handoff. Failed handoffs retry via RunFormHandoffJob with exponential backoff.

handoff_target_type.description: Polymorphic — the module::entity that received the handoff. E.g. `registrations::Registration` or `medical::MedicalClearance`.

handoff_target_id.description: The created entity's ID. Null until handoff succeeds.

ip_address_masked.description: GDPR-safe IPv4/IPv6 (last octet zeroed). For abuse detection + rate limiting only.

x-invariants: answers IMMUTABLE post-completed — only in_progress submissions accept PATCH.
Transitions: in_progress → completed / abandoned / failed_validation. completed / abandoned are terminal; failed_validation can retry back to in_progress.
handoff_target_type + handoff_target_id set together when handoff_status = 'succeeded'.
answers_hash set at status transition to `completed`.
